title: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
meta_description: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana 2026 — ₹2 lakh life insurance for ₹436/year covering death from any cause. Eligibility, enrollment, claim process, and comparison with PMSBY.

hero.one_liner: ₹2 lakh life insurance covering death from ANY cause — illness, accident, or natural — for just ₹436 per year (₹1.19/day)

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) is a government-backed pure term life insurance scheme launched on 9 May 2015. For an annual premium of ₹436 (about ₹1.19 per day), it provides ₹2 lakh life cover to the nominee in case of the policyholder's death from ANY cause — whether illness (heart attack, cancer, COVID), accident, or natural death.

This is different from PMSBY which covers only accidental death. PMJJBY covers ALL types of death — making it a true life insurance product. The scheme is available to anyone between 18-55 years with a savings bank account. Coverage continues until age 55, even if you enrolled at age 18 (total possible coverage: 37 years for just ₹436/year).

The premium of ₹436 is auto-debited from the linked bank account annually between May 25 and June 1. The coverage period runs from June 1 to May 31 each year. The scheme is underwritten by Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) and other life insurance companies partnered with participating banks.

As of 2026, over 16 crore people have enrolled in PMJJBY. The scheme has processed lakhs of claims, with an average claim settlement time of 30-60 days. For most low-income and middle-income families, the combination of PMJJBY (₹436) + PMSBY (₹20) provides ₹4 lakh total cover for just ₹456/year — possibly the cheapest comprehensive insurance in the world.

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: 18 to 55 years at enrollment. Coverage continues till age 55.

eligibility.label: Bank account
eligibility.value: Must have a savings bank account with Aadhaar linked

eligibility.label: Multiple banks
eligibility.value: Can enroll through only ONE bank, even if you have multiple accounts

eligibility.label: Premium
eligibility.value: ₹436/year — auto-debited annually between May 25 and June 1

eligibility.label: Health condition
eligibility.value: No medical exam required. No health declaration. Pre-existing conditions covered from day one.

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: No documents for enrollment — just sign consent form at bank. Only Aadhaar linkage needed.

extra_sections.heading: PMJJBY vs PMSBY vs Term Insurance — Complete Comparison
rows: Basic life cover for low-income families

extra_sections.heading: Why You Need PMJJBY Even If You Have Other Insurance
content: At ₹436/year, PMJJBY is practically free compared to the coverage it provides. Even if you have employer group insurance or a private term plan, PMJJBY is worth having because:
1. It's additional cover: PMJJBY payout is on top of any other insurance you have. If you die and have both PMJJBY and a private term plan, your family gets both payouts.

2. It never lapses due to job change: Employer insurance ends when you resign or are terminated. PMJJBY continues as long as you pay ₹436/year — it's linked to your personal bank account, not your employer.

3. No medical underwriting: If you develop a health condition (diabetes, heart disease) after enrolling, your coverage continues. Private insurers might reject your renewal or increase premiums — PMJJBY premium stays ₹436 regardless of health changes.

4. The math is absurd: ₹436/year for ₹2 lakh cover means the insurance company pays out 458× the premium if you die. No other insurance product offers this ratio. The scheme is heavily subsidized — the government bears the operational costs.

Recommendation: If you're between 18-55, enroll in BOTH PMJJBY (₹436) and PMSBY (₹20). Total cost: ₹456/year. Total cover: ₹4 lakh (₹2L for any death + ₹2L for accidental death/disability). If your death is due to accident, your family gets ₹4 lakh total. This is the bare minimum insurance every Indian adult should have.

steps.title: Nominee visits the bank
steps.description: Within 30 days of the policyholder's death, the nominee should visit the bank branch where PMJJBY was enrolled. Inform the bank and request the claim form.

steps.title: Submit required documents
steps.description: Death certificate (from municipal corporation), FIR (if applicable), PMJJBY enrollment proof or bank statement showing premium debit, nominee's Aadhaar card, nominee's bank account details for claim payment. For hospital death: discharge summary.

steps.title: Bank forwards to insurer
steps.description: The bank verifies documents and forwards the claim to the insurance company (LIC or partner insurer). The insurer reviews and processes the claim.

steps.title: ₹2 lakh credited to nominee
steps.description: Claim amount is credited to the nominee's bank account within 30-60 days of complete document submission. If rejected, written reasons are provided and can be escalated to the insurance ombudsman.

how_to_apply.title: Visit bank or use net banking/mobile banking
how_to_apply.description: Go to the bank where you have a savings account. Ask for PMJJBY enrollment. Most banks also allow enrollment through net banking or mobile app.

how_to_apply.title: Fill consent form
how_to_apply.description: One-page form — your details, nominee name and relationship, consent for ₹436 annual auto-debit.

how_to_apply.title: Premium debited in May-June
how_to_apply.description: ₹436 auto-debited annually. Ensure sufficient balance between May 25 and June 1.

how_to_apply.title: Coverage active from June 1
how_to_apply.description: Life cover of ₹2 lakh active from June 1 to May 31. Auto-renews each year via auto-debit.

important_alert.text: PMJJBY covers death from ALL causes (illness, accident, natural). For additional accident cover (₹2 lakh for just ₹20/year), also enroll in PMSBY. Both together = ₹456/year for ₹4 lakh total cover.

faqs.question: What if I die due to illness — will my family get the money?
faqs.answer: Yes. This is the key difference between PMJJBY and PMSBY. PMJJBY covers death from ANY cause — heart attack, cancer, kidney failure, COVID-19, accident, or any other reason. Your nominee gets ₹2 lakh regardless of the cause of death.

faqs.question: I'm 54 years old. Can I still enroll?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can enroll up to age 55. But your coverage will end on your 55th birthday. You'll get at most 1 year of coverage. The scheme is most valuable when enrolled young — a person enrolling at 18 gets 37 years of coverage. If you're 54, consider whether ₹436 for one year of ₹2 lakh cover is worthwhile for you.

faqs.question: Do I need a medical test?
faqs.answer: No. There is absolutely no medical examination, no health questionnaire, and no pre-existing condition exclusion. Everyone between 18-55 with a bank account is eligible regardless of health status. This makes PMJJBY particularly valuable for people with existing health conditions who might be rejected by private insurers.

faqs.question: Is there a waiting period?
faqs.answer: No. Coverage starts from June 1 (or from the date of enrollment if enrolled mid-year for the first time). There is no waiting period for any condition — you're fully covered from day one.

faqs.question: My premium wasn't debited this year. Am I still covered?
faqs.answer: No. If the ₹436 premium wasn't successfully debited from your account, your coverage lapses for that year. You have no insurance until the next successful premium payment. Re-enroll by visiting your bank and ensuring sufficient balance for the next debit cycle.

faqs.question: Can I increase the cover amount beyond ₹2 lakh?
faqs.answer: No. PMJJBY has a fixed cover of ₹2 lakh — it cannot be increased. If you need higher life cover (₹50 lakh+), buy a private term insurance plan alongside PMJJBY. Use PMJJBY as your base cover and private term insurance for adequate protection. See our Term Insurance Guide for more details.

faqs.question: Is the ₹436 premium tax-deductible?
faqs.answer: Yes. The PMJJBY premium of ₹436 qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. However, this is part of the overall ₹1.5 lakh limit shared with other 80C investments. Given the small amount, the tax benefit is marginal — but every bit counts.
